source_alert.description: Grid Overfrequ ency / Grid Overfrequency / Grid overfrequ ency. Cause ID = 1 Power grid exception: The actual grid frequency is higher than the requirement of the local power grid standard.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 Power grid exception: The actual grid frequency is higher than the requirement of the local power grid standard.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. If the alarm occurs accidentally, the power grid may be abnormal temporarily. The inverter automatically recovers after detecting that the power grid becomes normal. 2. If the alarm occurs frequently, check whether the grid frequency is within the acceptable range. If no, contact the local power operator. If yes, log in to the mobile phone app, SmartLogger, or NMS to modify the grid underfrequency protection threshold with the consent of the local power operator.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Grid Underfreq uency / Grid Underfrequenc y / Grid underfreq uency / Grid Underfrequency
source_alert.description: Grid Underfreq uency / Grid Underfrequenc y / Grid underfreq uency / Grid Underfrequency. Cause ID = 1 Power grid exception: The actual grid frequency is lower than the requirement of the local power grid standard.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 Power grid exception: The actual grid frequency is lower than the requirement of the local power grid standard.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. If the alarm occurs accidentally, the power grid may be abnormal temporarily. The inverter automatically recovers after detecting that the power grid becomes normal. 2. If the alarm occurs frequently, check whether the grid frequency is within the acceptable range. If no, contact the local power operator. If yes, log in to the mobile phone app, SmartLogger, or NMS to modify the grid underfrequency protection threshold with the consent of the local power operator.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Unstable Grid Frequency / Unstable grid frequency
source_alert.description: Unstable Grid Frequency / Unstable grid frequency. Cause ID = 1 Power grid exception: The actual grid frequency change rate does not comply with the local power grid standard.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 Power grid exception: The actual grid frequency change rate does not comply with the local power grid standard.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. If the alarm occurs occasionally, the power grid may be abnormal temporarily. The SUN2000 automatically recovers after detecting that the power grid becomes normal. 2. If the alarm occurs frequently, check whether the power grid frequency is within the allowed range. If no, contact the local power operator.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Output Overcurre nt / Output Overcurrent / Output overcurre nt
source_alert.description: Output Overcurre nt / Output Overcurrent / Output overcurre nt. Cause ID = 1 The grid voltage drops dramatically or the power grid is short-circuited. As a result, the inverter transient output current exceeds the upper threshold, and inverter protection is triggered.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The grid voltage drops dramatically or the power grid is short-circuited. As a result, the inverter transient output current exceeds the upper threshold, and inverter protection is triggered.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. The inverter monitors its external working conditions in real time. The inverter automatically recovers after the fault is rectified. 2. If the alarm occurs frequently and affects the power production of the PV plant, check whether the output is short-circuited. If the fault persists, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Output DC Componen t Overhigh / Output DC Component Overhigh / Output DC compone nt overhigh
source_alert.description: Output DC Componen t Overhigh / Output DC Component Overhigh / Output DC compone nt overhigh. Cause ID = 1 The DC component in the grid current exceeds the upper threshold.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The DC component in the grid current exceeds the upper threshold.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. The inverter monitors its external working conditions in real time. The inverter automatically recovers after the fault is rectified. 2. If the alarm occurs frequently, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Abnormal Residual Current / Abnormal residual current
source_alert.description: Abnormal Residual Current / Abnormal residual current. Cause ID = 1 The input-to- ground insulation impedance has decreased during the inverter operation.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The input-to- ground insulation impedance has decreased during the inverter operation.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. If the alarm occurs accidentally, the external power cable may be abnormal temporarily. The inverter automatically recovers after the fault is rectified. 2. If the alarm occurs frequently or persists, check that the impedance between the PV string and ground is not below the lower threshold.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Abnormal Grounding / Abnormal groundin g
source_alert.description: Abnormal Grounding / Abnormal groundin g. Cause ID = 1 ● The neutral wire or PE cable of the inverter is not connected. ● The output mode set for the inverter is inconsistent with the cable connection mode.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● The neutral wire or PE cable of the inverter is not connected. ● The output mode set for the inverter is inconsistent with the cable connection mode.
source_alert.recommended_action: Power off the inverter (turn off the AC output switch and DC input switch, and wait for a period of time. For details about the wait time, see the description on the device safety warning label), and then perform the following operations: 1. Check whether the PE cable for the inverter is connected properly. 2. If the inverter is connected to a TN power grid, check whether the neutral wire is properly connected and whether the voltage of the neutral wire to ground is normal. 3. After the inverter is powered on, check whether the output mode set for the inverter is consistent with the output cable connection mode.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Low Insulation Resistance / Low insulation resistance
source_alert.description: Low Insulation Resistance / Low insulation resistance. Cause ID = 1 ● A short circuit occurs between the PV array and the ground. ● The ambient air of the PV array is damp and the insulation between the PV array and the ground is poor.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● A short circuit occurs between the PV array and the ground. ● The ambient air of the PV array is damp and the insulation between the PV array and the ground is poor.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Check the output impedance of the PV array to ground. If there is a short circuit or lack of insulation, rectify it. 2. Check that the PE cable of the SUN2000 is correctly connected. 3. If the impedance is lower than the specified protection threshold in rainy and cloudy days, set Insulation resistance protection using the mobile app, SmartLogger, or NMS. Current insulation resistance: x MOhm, possible short circuit position: x%. The short circuit position is valid for a single PV string. If there are multiple PV strings, check the PV strings one by one. For details, see F Locating Insulation Resistance Faults. For details about how to query the low insulation resistance alarm, see HUAWEI SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 and SUN6000-5/6KTL- L0 Inverter Low Insulation Resistance Fault Indication Guide.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Overtemperature / Overtemperatu re / Cabinet overtemp erature / Cabinet Overtemperatur e
source_alert.description: Overtemperature / Overtemperatu re / Cabinet overtemp erature / Cabinet Overtemperatur e. Cause ID = 1 ● The inverter is installed in a place with poor ventilation. ● The ambient temperature exceeds the upper threshold. ● The inverter is not working properly.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● The inverter is installed in a place with poor ventilation. ● The ambient temperature exceeds the upper threshold. ● The inverter is not working properly.
source_alert.recommended_action: ● Check the ventilation and ambient temperature at the inverter installation position. ● If the ventilation is poor or the ambient temperature exceeds the upper threshold, improve the ventilation and heat dissipation. ● If the ventilation and ambient temperature both meet requirements, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Device Fault / Equipme nt fault
source_alert.description: Device Fault / Equipme nt fault. Cause ID = 1–12 An unrecoverable fault has occurred on a circuit inside the inverter.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1–12 An unrecoverable fault has occurred on a circuit inside the inverter.
source_alert.recommended_action: Turn off the AC output switch and DC input switch, and then turn them on after 5 minutes. If the fault persists, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support. Note: If the cause ID is ID 1, perform the preceding operation when the PV string current is less than 1 A.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Upgrade Failed / Upgrade Failed or Version Mismatch / Upgrade failed or version mismatch
source_alert.description: Upgrade Failed / Upgrade Failed or Version Mismatch / Upgrade failed or version mismatch. Cause ID = 1–4 The upgrade does not end normally.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1–4 The upgrade does not end normally.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Perform an upgrade again. 2. If the upgrade fails several times, contact your supplier. 3. Check that the DC power supply is connected.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: License Expired
source_alert.description: License Expired. Cause ID = 1 ● The privilege certificate has entered the grace period. ● The privilege feature will be invalid soon.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● The privilege certificate has entered the grace period. ● The privilege feature will be invalid soon.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Apply for a new certificate. 2. Load the new certificate.

source_alert.name: Faulty Power Collector
source_alert.description: Faulty Power Collector. Cause ID = 1 The smart power sensor is disconnected.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The smart power sensor is disconnected.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Check that the configured power meter model is the same as the actual model. 2. Check that the communications parameters for the smart power sensors are the same as the SUN2000 RS485 configurations. 3. Check whether the smart power sensor is powered on and whether the RS485 communications cable is connected.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Battery Abnormal / Battery abnormal
source_alert.description: Battery Abnormal / Battery abnormal. The battery is faulty, disconnected, or the battery circuit breaker is OFF when the battery is running. Cause ID = 1–4 ● 1: The battery communication is abnormal. ● 2: The battery port experiences overcurrent. ● 3. The battery enabling cable is not properly connected. ● 4: The battery port voltage is abnormal.
source_alert.probable_cause: The battery is faulty, disconnected, or the battery circuit breaker is OFF when the battery is running. Cause ID = 1–4 ● 1: The battery communication is abnormal. ● 2: The battery port experiences overcurrent. ● 3. The battery enabling cable is not properly connected. ● 4: The battery port voltage is abnormal.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. If the battery fault indicator is steady on or blinking, contact the battery supplier. 2. Check that the battery enabling/power/ communications cable is correctly installed, and that the communications parameters are the same as the SUN2000 RS485 configurations. 3. Check that the auxiliary power switch on the battery is set to ON. 4. Send a shutdown command on the app. Turn off the AC output switch, DC input switch, and battery switch. Then turn on the battery switch, AC output switch, and DC input switch in sequence after 5 minutes. 5. If the fault persists, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Active Islanding
source_alert.description: Active Islanding. Cause ID = 1 When the power grid experiences an AC power outage, the SUN2000 detects islanding proactively.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 When the power grid experiences an AC power outage, the SUN2000 detects islanding proactively.
source_alert.recommended_action: Check that the grid connection voltage of the SUN2000 is normal.

source_alert.name: Transient AC overvolta ge / Transient AC Overvoltage
source_alert.description: Transient AC overvolta ge / Transient AC Overvoltage. Cause ID = 1 The inverter detects that the phase voltage exceeds the transient AC overvoltage protection threshold.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The inverter detects that the phase voltage exceeds the transient AC overvoltage protection threshold.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Check whether the grid connection voltage exceeds the upper threshold. If yes, contact the local power operator. 2. If you have confirmed that the grid connection voltage exceeds the upper threshold and obtained the consent of the local power operator, modify the overvoltage protection threshold. 3. Check that the peak grid voltage does not exceed the upper threshold.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

source_alert.name: Peripheral Port Short Circuit
source_alert.description: Peripheral Port Short Circuit. Cause ID = 1 The SUN2000L Alarm port is short-circuited when supplying 12 V power to peripheral.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The SUN2000L Alarm port is short-circuited when supplying 12 V power to peripheral.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Turn off the AC and DC switches on the SUN2000L and disconnect the peripheral from the Alarm port. Then power on the SUN2000L again and clear the alarm manually. If the alarm persists, contact your supplier to rectify the fault. If the alarm is cleared, it indicates that the port on the SUN2000L is normal. Then check that the pins in the power port on the peripheral are not short-circuited. 2. Replace the peripheral, and then start a self-check to check that the port functions properly.

source_alert.name: Off-grid output overload
source_alert.description: Off-grid output overload. Cause ID = 1/2 The output is overloaded or short-circuited.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1/2 The output is overloaded or short-circuited.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Check whether the device output is short- circuited. 2. Check whether the device load configuration exceeds the rated value.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Abnormal PV Module Configuration / Abnormal PV module configuration
source_alert.description: Abnormal PV Module Configuration / Abnormal PV module configuration. ● Cause ID = 1 The number of optimizers connected to the inverter exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 2 The PV string power or the number of optimizers connected in series in a PV string exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 3 The number of optimizers connected in series in a PV string is less than the lower threshold, the PV string output is reversely connected, or the output of some optimizers in the PV string is reversely connected. ● Cause ID = 4 The number of PV strings connected to the inverter exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 5 The PV string output is reversely connected or the PV string output is short-circuited. ● Cause ID = 6 Under the same MPPT, the number of optimizers connected in series in PV strings connected in parallel is different, or the output of some optimizers in PV strings is reversely connected. ● Cause ID = 7 The optimizer installation position is changed, or PV strings are combined or exchanged. ● Cause ID = 8 The sunlight is weak or changes abnormally. ● Cause ID = 9 In partial configuration scenarios, the PV string voltage exceeds the inverter input voltage specifications.
source_alert.probable_cause: ● Cause ID = 1 The number of optimizers connected to the inverter exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 2 The PV string power or the number of optimizers connected in series in a PV string exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 3 The number of optimizers connected in series in a PV string is less than the lower threshold, the PV string output is reversely connected, or the output of some optimizers in the PV string is reversely connected. ● Cause ID = 4 The number of PV strings connected to the inverter exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 5 The PV string output is reversely connected or the PV string output is short-circuited. ● Cause ID = 6 Under the same MPPT, the number of optimizers connected in series in PV strings connected in parallel is different, or the output of some optimizers in PV strings is reversely connected. ● Cause ID = 7 The optimizer installation position is changed, or PV strings are combined or exchanged. ● Cause ID = 8 The sunlight is weak or changes abnormally. ● Cause ID = 9 In partial configuration scenarios, the PV string voltage exceeds the inverter input voltage specifications.
source_alert.recommended_action: Check whether the total number of PV modules, number of PV modules in a PV string, and number of PV strings meet requirements and whether the PV module output is reversely connected. ● Cause ID 1: Check whether the total number of optimizers exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID 2: Check whether the PV string power or the number of PV strings connected in series exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID 3: 1. Check whether the number of optimizers connected in series in the PV string is below the lower threshold. 2. Check whether the PV string output is reversely connected. 3. Check whether the PV string output is disconnected. 4. Check whether the optimizer output extension cable is correct (positive connector at one end and negative connector at the other). ● Cause ID 4: Check whether the number of PV strings exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID 5: Check whether the PV string output is reversely connected or short- circuited. ● Cause ID 6: 1. Check whether the number of optimizers connected in series in the PV strings connected in parallel under the same MPPT is the same. 2. Check whether the optimizer output extension cable is correct (positive connector at one end and negative connector at the other). ● Cause ID 7: When the sunlight is normal, perform the optimizer search function again. ● Cause ID 8: When the sunlight is normal, perform the optimizer search function again. ● Cause ID 9: Calculate the PV string voltage based on the number of PV modules in the string and check whether the string voltage exceeds the upper threshold of the inverter input voltage.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

source_alert.name: Optimizer Fault / Optimize r fault
source_alert.description: Optimizer Fault / Optimize r fault. Cause ID = 1 An optimizer is faulty.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 An optimizer is faulty.
source_alert.recommended_action: Go to the optimizer information page to view the fault information.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

source_alert.name: Grid-tied/Off- grid Controller Abnormal / Grid-tied/ Off-grid Controlle r Abnormal
source_alert.description: Grid-tied/Off- grid Controller Abnormal / Grid-tied/ Off-grid Controlle r Abnormal. Cause ID = 1 The inverter fails to communicate with the Smart Backup Box. Cause ID = 2 An unrecoverable fault occurs on a circuit inside the Smart Backup Box.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The inverter fails to communicate with the Smart Backup Box. Cause ID = 2 An unrecoverable fault occurs on a circuit inside the Smart Backup Box.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Send a shutdown command on the app. Turn off the AC output switch, DC input switch, and battery switch. 2. Check whether the power cable and RS485 cable between the Smart Backup Box and the inverter are normal. 3. After 5 minutes, turn on the battery switch, AC output side, AC output switch, and DC input switch. 4. If the alarm persists, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: PV String Abnormal
source_alert.description: Power yield is lower than expected based on current light.
source_alert.probable_cause: Power yield is lower than expected based on current light.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Clean the PV modules (dirt/bird droppings).
2. Check for new shading (trees/antennas).
3. Use "IV Curve Diagnosis" in the app to check for micro-cracks or cell degradation.

source_alert.name: ESS Communication Failure
source_alert.description: RS485 communication cable is disconnected, loose, or interference is too high.
source_alert.probable_cause: RS485 communication cable is disconnected, loose, or interference is too high.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check the RJ45 or terminal connections at the inverter COM port and battery control unit.
2. Verify that Pins 1/2 or 7/9 are correctly crimped (Huawei uses specific pins, not standard T568B).
3. Ensure the communication cable is shielded and grounded at one end.
4. Restart the communication dongle/SmartLogger to refresh the Modbus loop.

source_alert.name: ESS Abnormal Behavior
source_alert.description: Internal software logic error or conflicting commands from the SmartLogger.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal software logic error or conflicting commands from the SmartLogger.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Perform a firmware update for both the Inverter and the Battery Control Module.
2. Check if there are third-party EMS systems sending conflicting Modbus commands.
3. Restart the battery DC switch.

source_alert.name: Battery Low Voltage
source_alert.description: The battery has reached 0% SOC and remained there too long, entering "hibernation."
source_alert.probable_cause: The battery has reached 0% SOC and remained there too long, entering "hibernation."
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Immediate: Press the "Black Start" button on the side of the battery for 5 seconds.
2. Enable "Emergency Charge from Grid" in the FusionSolar app settings.
3. Ensure the battery DC switch is ON. If it won't stay ON, the voltage is too low for the internal relay to hold.

source_alert.name: Battery Module Fault
source_alert.description: Internal hardware failure in one of the 5kWh/7kWh power modules.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal hardware failure in one of the 5kWh/7kWh power modules.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Observe the LED rings on each battery module; a steady red ring identifies the faulty pack.
2. Turn off the DC switch, wait 5 minutes, and restart.
3. If the error persists, isolate the faulty module (disconnect its DC/Comm links) to allow the rest of the stack to operate.

source_alert.name: Battery System Overload
source_alert.description: Discharge power exceeds the maximum rating of the battery modules.
source_alert.probable_cause: Discharge power exceeds the maximum rating of the battery modules.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check if high-power appliances (motors, pumps) started simultaneously while off-grid.
2. Add more battery modules to the stack to increase the maximum discharge power (P_{max}).
3. Review your "Backup Power" settings to limit discharge during outages.

source_alert.name: Battery Overvoltage
source_alert.description: Charging voltage exceeds safety limits (usually a BMS calibration issue).
source_alert.probable_cause: Charging voltage exceeds safety limits (usually a BMS calibration issue).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. The system usually recovers automatically. If not, perform a "Battery SOC Calibration" in the app maintenance menu.
2. Check for firmware updates that address BMS charging logic.

source_alert.name: Battery Overcurrent
source_alert.description: Sudden spike in current during charging or discharging.
source_alert.probable_cause: Sudden spike in current during charging or discharging.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for short circuits in the DC cables between the inverter and battery.
2. The system will attempt to auto-recover 3 times. If it fails, a manual restart is required.
3. Inspect the DC fuse inside the Battery Control Unit (BCU).

source_alert.name: Battery Overtemperature
source_alert.description: High ambient temperature; blocked air vents; direct sunlight.
source_alert.probable_cause: High ambient temperature; blocked air vents; direct sunlight.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ensure at least 15cm of clearance around all sides of the battery modules.
2. Clean dust from the vents of the Control Unit and modules.
3. Install a sunshield if the battery is outdoors and exposed to direct sun.
4. Check if the internal cooling fan is blocked or faulty.

source_alert.name: Battery Undertemperature
source_alert.description: Temperature dropped below -10^{°}C (for S0) or -20^{°}C (for S1).
source_alert.probable_cause: Temperature dropped below -10^{°}C (for S0) or -20^{°}C (for S1).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. In cold climates, ensure the internal "Heating Film" is enabled in settings (for S1 models).
2. If the battery is in an uninsulated garage, consider adding basic insulation or a heat source.
3. The battery will stop discharging/charging to protect the cells until it warms up.

source_alert.name: Battery Pack Locked
source_alert.description: A major safety fault has occurred, and the BMS has locked the pack for safety.
source_alert.probable_cause: A major safety fault has occurred, and the BMS has locked the pack for safety.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. This usually follows a severe Overcurrent or Overvoltage event.
2. Check the "Cause ID" for the specific safety trigger.
3. Requires an installer to "Unlock" the device via the local commissioning tool after verifying wiring.

source_alert.name: BMS Communication Error
source_alert.description: Internal communication failure between the Control Unit (BCU) and the modules.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal communication failure between the Control Unit (BCU) and the modules.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check the internal "Link" cables between the stacked battery modules.
2. Ensure the modules are physically seated correctly and the side locking bolts are tight.
3. Inspect the pins on the module-to-module connectors for bends or corrosion.

source_alert.name: ESS Anti-Island Fault
source_alert.description: Detected during off-grid/backup mode; grid signal is bleeding through.
source_alert.probable_cause: Detected during off-grid/backup mode; grid signal is bleeding through.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check the "Backup Box" or "SmartGuard" wiring. Ensure the Neutral-Earth bond is correct for your region.
2. Verify the Main Switch is fully open during a grid failure to prevent "back-feeding."

source_alert.name: ESS DC Switch Off
source_alert.description: The physical DC switch on the side of the battery is turned OFF.
source_alert.probable_cause: The physical DC switch on the side of the battery is turned OFF.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Manually flip the switch to ON.
2. If it immediately flips back to OFF, there is a short circuit or a "Critical Fault" (see 3107) preventing startup.

source_alert.name: Monitoring Unit Faulty / Faulty monitorin g unit / Faulty Monitoring Unit
source_alert.description: Monitoring Unit Faulty / Faulty monitorin g unit / Faulty Monitoring Unit. Cause ID = 1 ● The flash memory is insufficient. ● The flash memory has bad sectors.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● The flash memory is insufficient. ● The flash memory has bad sectors.
source_alert.recommended_action: Turn off the AC output switch and DC input switch, and then turn them on after 5 minutes. If the alarm persists, replace the monitoring board or contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
source_alert.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

source_alert.name: Internal Error
source_alert.description: Metrology chip failure or voltage spike damage.
source_alert.probable_cause: Metrology chip failure or voltage spike damage.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Restart the meter by flipping its breaker.
2. If "Err" persists, the meter hardware is damaged and needs replacement.

source_alert.name: Minor/Warning
source_alert.description: Minor communication lag or environmental warning.
source_alert.probable_cause: Minor communication lag or environmental warning.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for "String Abnormal" or "Grid Frequency" warnings.
2. Often clears itself; if it persists, check the RS485 cabling for noise.

source_alert.name: Major Alarm
source_alert.description: Critical device offline (Inverter or Battery) or internal fault.
source_alert.probable_cause: Critical device offline (Inverter or Battery) or internal fault.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Log into the SmartLogger WebUI (Enspire).
2. Navigate to "Active Alarms" to see which specific device is failing.
3. Check the "Maintenance" tab for hardware self-test results.

source_alert.name: Offline
source_alert.description: The optimizer is not communicating with the inverter.
source_alert.probable_cause: The optimizer is not communicating with the inverter.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Search: Run "Optimizer Search" from the Inverter Maintenance menu.
2. Sunlight: Ensure there is enough irradiance (>50 W/m^2) to "wake up" the optimizer.
3. Distance: Check if the string length or cable distance exceeds the PLC communication limit.
4. SN Check: Verify the Serial Number (SN) in the physical layout matches the actual device.

source_alert.name: Overvoltage
source_alert.description: Panel V_{oc} is higher than the optimizer's limit (e.g., >80V for 600W models).
source_alert.probable_cause: Panel V_{oc} is higher than the optimizer's limit (e.g., >80V for 600W models).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Compare the panel datasheet (V_{oc} at min. temp) with the optimizer’s maximum input voltage.
2. If you are using "2-in-1" wiring (two panels to one 1100W optimizer), ensure they are in parallel, not series, if voltage is the issue.

source_alert.name: Reverse CTs
source_alert.description: Current Transformers (CTs) installed backwards.
source_alert.probable_cause: Current Transformers (CTs) installed backwards.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check the arrow on the CTs; it must point toward the load (house).
2. Ensure Phase L1 CT is on Phase L1 wire (mixing phases causes wrong data).

source_alert.name: Scan Failure
source_alert.description: RS485 loop is open or addresses are duplicated.
source_alert.probable_cause: RS485 loop is open or addresses are duplicated.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ensure every inverter in the chain has a unique RS485 address (1, 2, 3...).
2. Check the 120\Omega termination resistor switch at the end of the long RS485 run.
3. Measure voltage on the RS485 line (should be approx. 2-5V DC).

source_alert.name: Power Loss
source_alert.description: Blown fuse or incorrect wiring to terminals 1/2/3.
source_alert.probable_cause: Blown fuse or incorrect wiring to terminals 1/2/3.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Use a multimeter to check for voltage at the meter's input terminals.
2. Ensure the protection fuses for the meter haven't tripped.

source_alert.name: Backfeed
source_alert.description: Current is flowing backward into the optimizer (common in parallel strings).
source_alert.probable_cause: Current is flowing backward into the optimizer (common in parallel strings).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for severe shading on one string vs. another on the same MPPT.
2. Inspect the string for mismatched panel types or counts.
3. Verify all strings on the same MPPT have the same number of optimizers.

source_alert.name: Overheat
source_alert.description: Ambient heat is too high or airflow behind the panel is blocked.
source_alert.probable_cause: Ambient heat is too high or airflow behind the panel is blocked.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ensure the optimizer is mounted to the racking rail, not directly against the hot panel backsheet.
2. Check for bird nests or debris blocking airflow behind the modules.
3. The device will auto-resume once it cools below 70°\text{C}.

source_alert.name: SIM/Comm Error
source_alert.description: No SIM card (4G) or no Router connection (WLAN).
source_alert.probable_cause: No SIM card (4G) or no Router connection (WLAN).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. 4G: Verify the SIM card is inserted correctly and has an active data plan.
2. WLAN: Ensure the router is ON and within range (no more than 10-15m).
3. Re-run the "Quick Settings" wizard in the App.

source_alert.name: Cloud Link Error
source_alert.description: Connected to router but can't reach Huawei Cloud.
source_alert.probable_cause: Connected to router but can't reach Huawei Cloud.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check if the router has internet access.
2. Ensure Firewall ports 27250 and 16100 are open.
3. Check if the DNS is set to a reliable source (like 8.8.8.8).

source_alert.name: Dongle Fault
source_alert.description: Internal hardware failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal hardware failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Remove the Dongle and re-insert it firmly.
2. Check if the USB port on the inverter is clean/dry.
3. If it stays red on multiple inverters, the Dongle needs replacement.

source_alert.name: Faulty
source_alert.description: Internal hardware failure or critical safety trip.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal hardware failure or critical safety trip.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Reset: Power cycle the DC and AC of the inverter and wait 5 minutes.
2. Isolate: If the red icon persists, swap the "faulty" optimizer with a known working one nearby to see if the fault follows the device or the panel.

source_alert.name: No Inverter Comm
source_alert.description: Dongle not talking to the Inverter.
source_alert.probable_cause: Dongle not talking to the Inverter.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ensure the Inverter's RS485 settings match the Dongle (standard is Baud 9600).
2. Check if the Dongle is compatible with that specific inverter model.

source_alert.name: Firmware Error
source_alert.description: Communication was interrupted during a software update.
source_alert.probable_cause: Communication was interrupted during a software update.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ensure sunlight is stable (don't update at dusk).
2. Move the mobile device closer to the Smart Dongle/Logger during the update.
3. Retry the update; the optimizer will keep its old firmware until a "Success" is reached.

source_alert.name: Disconnected
source_alert.description: The optimizer was seen before but is now missing from the string loop.
source_alert.probable_cause: The optimizer was seen before but is now missing from the string loop.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Physical Link: Check for a loose MC4 connector or a cut cable in the string.
2. Bypass: Check if the optimizer has entered "Bypass Mode" due to a severe panel mismatch.

source_alert.name: Internal Error
source_alert.description: MCU or Control Logic fault.
source_alert.probable_cause: MCU or Control Logic fault.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Hard reboot (AC/DC off).
2. Verify firmware version.
3. Check internal logs for specific sub-codes.

source_alert.name: EEPROM Error
source_alert.description: Memory corruption or R/W failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Memory corruption or R/W failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Factory reset.
2. Reflash firmware.
3. Reconfigure parameters manually.

source_alert.name: Relay Fault
source_alert.description: Relay stuck (open or closed).
source_alert.probable_cause: Relay stuck (open or closed).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Listen for the physical "click" during startup.
2. Check AC grid stability/voltage.

source_alert.name: Current Sensor
source_alert.description: Hall effect sensor failure or offset.
source_alert.probable_cause: Hall effect sensor failure or offset.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Inspect sensor wiring harness.
2. Compare internal readings vs. external clamp meter.

source_alert.name: Hardware Fault
source_alert.description: General PCB/Internal failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: General PCB/Internal failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Visual inspection for "magic smoke" or bulging caps.
2. Check for thermal hotspots on the board.

source_alert.name: IGBT Fault
source_alert.description: Power module/Gate driver failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Power module/Gate driver failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Caution: Check DC insulation before restarting.
2. Inspect cooling/thermal paste integrity.

source_alert.name: Fan Fault
source_alert.description: Cooling obstruction or motor failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cooling obstruction or motor failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Manual rotation check (debris).
2. Test 12V/24V power supply to the fan header.

source_alert.name: Relay Failure
source_alert.description: Physical relay damage (pitted contacts).
source_alert.probable_cause: Physical relay damage (pitted contacts).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Continuity test across poles (when de-energized).
2. Replace relay or power board.

source_alert.name: Calibration Error
source_alert.description: Sensor drift or reference voltage loss.
source_alert.probable_cause: Sensor drift or reference voltage loss.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Attempt manual recalibration via service software.
2. Verify internal DC bus voltage accuracy.

source_alert.name: DSP Error
source_alert.description: Digital Signal Processor sync issue.
source_alert.probable_cause: Digital Signal Processor sync issue.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Firmware update.
2. Check for EMI/interference from nearby high-power lines.

source_alert.name: Grid Over Voltage
source_alert.description: Local grid high or "Voltage Rise" in AC cables.
source_alert.probable_cause: Local grid high or "Voltage Rise" in AC cables.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Measure AC voltage at inverter terminals.
2. Check AC cable gauge: Undersized wires cause voltage rise.
3. Verify Grid Standard (e.g., IEEE 1547).

source_alert.name: Grid Under Voltage
source_alert.description: Grid sag or loose AC connections.
source_alert.probable_cause: Grid sag or loose AC connections.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for loose terminals or tripped AC breakers.
2. Inspect transformer tap settings if site-wide.

source_alert.name: Over Frequency
source_alert.description: Grid instability or Generator "drift."
source_alert.probable_cause: Grid instability or Generator "drift."
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. If using a backup generator, check the governor/Hz.
2. Monitor for utility-side frequency fluctuations.

source_alert.name: Grid Loss
source_alert.description: Total loss of AC sync.
source_alert.probable_cause: Total loss of AC sync.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Confirm AC breaker is ON.
2. Check for "Island Mode" or blackout in the area.

source_alert.name: Avg. V-Rise/Fall
source_alert.description: 10-minute average voltage drift.
source_alert.probable_cause: 10-minute average voltage drift.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Monitor voltage over a 24hr cycle.
2. Contact utility if average voltage consistently exceeds limits.

source_alert.name: Phase Loss
source_alert.description: Missing L1, L2, or L3.
source_alert.probable_cause: Missing L1, L2, or L3.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Measure phase-to-phase and phase-to-neutral.
2. Check for blown fuses in the AC disconnect.

source_alert.name: Grid Unbalance
source_alert.description: Asymmetrical phase voltages.
source_alert.probable_cause: Asymmetrical phase voltages.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for heavy single-phase loads on the site.
2. Verify all AC terminal torque settings.

source_alert.name: AC Over-Current / Short
source_alert.description: External fault or "Short" to ground.
source_alert.probable_cause: External fault or "Short" to ground.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. STOP: Disconnect AC and Megger the AC cables.
2. Check for "nicked" insulation or water in the conduit.

source_alert.name: Anti-Islanding
source_alert.description: Protection trip.
source_alert.probable_cause: Protection trip.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Verify grid stability.
2. Check if multiple inverters are "fighting" on one bus.

source_alert.name: Grid Code Error
source_alert.description: Incorrect regional settings.
source_alert.probable_cause: Incorrect regional settings.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Access the Safety Parameter menu.
2. Select the correct Country/Grid code for the region.

source_alert.name: PV Over-Voltage
source_alert.description: String V_{oc} exceeds inverter Max Input.
source_alert.probable_cause: String V_{oc} exceeds inverter Max Input.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Danger: Disconnect DC immediately.
2. Measure V_{oc} of the string.
3. Recalculate for cold weather expansion.

source_alert.name: PV Under-Voltage
source_alert.description: String voltage below Startup/MPPT range.
source_alert.probable_cause: String voltage below Startup/MPPT range.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for undersized strings (too few panels).
2. Verify all MC4 connectors are fully seated.

source_alert.name: Insulation / Isolation
source_alert.description: Leakage current to ground (often moisture).
source_alert.probable_cause: Leakage current to ground (often moisture).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Perform an Insulation Resistance Test (Megger).
2. Inspect for "nicked" cables or water in MC4s.

source_alert.name: Reverse Polarity
source_alert.description: DC Positive/Negative swapped.
source_alert.probable_cause: DC Positive/Negative swapped.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Use a multimeter to verify +/- at the inverter input.
2. Correct the wiring at the string level.

source_alert.name: PV No Input
source_alert.description: Open circuit or disconnected DC.
source_alert.probable_cause: Open circuit or disconnected DC.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Verify DC Isolator/Switch is ON.
2. Check for blown string fuses (if applicable).

source_alert.name: Weak Power
source_alert.description: Low irradiance or extreme shading.
source_alert.probable_cause: Low irradiance or extreme shading.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for heavy snow, dust, or "hard shading" (trees).
2. Verify if the inverter is stuck in "Waiting" mode.

source_alert.name: String Mismatch
source_alert.description: Imbalance between parallel strings.
source_alert.probable_cause: Imbalance between parallel strings.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ensure all strings on the same MPPT have the same panel count.
2. Check for mismatched panel models.

source_alert.name: PV Ground Fault
source_alert.description: Direct short between DC and Earth.
source_alert.probable_cause: Direct short between DC and Earth.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Inspect the racking and grounding lugs.
2. Check if a cable is pinched under a module frame.

source_alert.name: PV Current Error
source_alert.description: Internal sensor drift or Hall fault.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal sensor drift or Hall fault.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Power cycle the inverter.
2. If it persists without load, the internal sensor is likely faulty.

source_alert.name: DC Bus Over-Voltage
source_alert.description: Internal DC Bus spike.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal DC Bus spike.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Restart inverter immediately.
2. Compare PV input voltage with the Max DC Bus rating.
3. Check for external grid surges pushing back.

source_alert.name: DC Bus Under-Voltage
source_alert.description: Internal Bus cannot maintain charge.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal Bus cannot maintain charge.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Verify PV input is sufficient for the "Startup" threshold.
2. Check for failing internal boost converters.

source_alert.name: Bus Unbalance
source_alert.description: Split-capacitor voltage mismatch.
source_alert.probable_cause: Split-capacitor voltage mismatch.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Power cycle to re-balance.
2. Visual Inspection: Look for "bulging" or leaking electrolytic capacitors.

source_alert.name: Soft Start Fail
source_alert.description: Pre-charge circuit failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Pre-charge circuit failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ensure DC input is stable during the 60s countdown.
2. If it fails repeatedly at 0 seconds, the soft-start resistor is likely blown.

source_alert.name: Over-Current
source_alert.description: Sudden internal/external demand.
source_alert.probable_cause: Sudden internal/external demand.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Inspect AC and DC wiring for intermittent shorts.
2. Restart to clear "phantom" current spikes.

source_alert.name: Short Circuit
source_alert.description: Critical output/IGBT short.
source_alert.probable_cause: Critical output/IGBT short.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. STOP: Disconnect AC load and PV strings.
2. Inspect AC terminals for carbon tracking (arc marks).
3. Replace inverter if the fault clears only when loads are removed.

source_alert.name: Internal Comm.
source_alert.description: Ribbon cable or internal bus fault.
source_alert.probable_cause: Ribbon cable or internal bus fault.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Restart inverter.
2. If persistent, internal board-to-board cables may be loose.

source_alert.name: WiFi Module
source_alert.description: Hardware or sync failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Hardware or sync failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Reseat the WiFi dongle/module.
2. Check for "Steady Blue/Green" LED on the module.

source_alert.name: Server Fail
source_alert.description: DNS or Firewall blockage.
source_alert.probable_cause: DNS or Firewall blockage.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ping the inverter's IP from a local laptop.
2. Check if the router is blocking Ports 80, 443, or 502.

source_alert.name: RS485 Error
source_alert.description: Wiring polarity or termination.
source_alert.probable_cause: Wiring polarity or termination.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Swap A/B wires if using a 3rd party logger.
2. Check for 120Ohm termination resistor at the end of the chain.

source_alert.name: CAN Fault
source_alert.description: BMS handshake failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: BMS handshake failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Battery comm cable: Ensure it is a "Straight-through" or "Crossover" as required.
2. Verify Battery ID/Address settings.

source_alert.name: Logger Fault
source_alert.description: External data logger power.
source_alert.probable_cause: External data logger power.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Verify the logger is receiving 5V/12V DC power.
2. Check for firmware compatibility between logger and inverter.

source_alert.name: Data Storage
source_alert.description: Flash memory "Read/Write" error.
source_alert.probable_cause: Flash memory "Read/Write" error.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Attempt a System Reset to clear the cache.
2. Reflash firmware to re-partition the memory.

source_alert.name: Battery Over-Voltage
source_alert.description: Charge limit exceeded.
source_alert.probable_cause: Charge limit exceeded.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Verify "Max Charge Voltage" in inverter settings matches the battery datasheet.
2. Check for BMS "Cell Imbalance" causing a total pack spike.

source_alert.name: Battery Under-Voltage
source_alert.description: Voltage below "Cut-off."
source_alert.probable_cause: Voltage below "Cut-off."
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. If the battery is "asleep," use a Force Charge or "Jumpstart" if supported.
2. Increase the "Minimum SOC" (State of Charge) to 15-20% to prevent deep discharge.

source_alert.name: BMS Comm Lost
source_alert.description: Handshake failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Handshake failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check the RJ45/CAN cable pinout (T-568B vs. Custom).
2. Verify the Battery ID/Dip-switch settings on the master battery.

source_alert.name: Battery Temp Fault
source_alert.description: Thermal limit reached.
source_alert.probable_cause: Thermal limit reached.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. High Temp: Improve ventilation/cooling in the battery room.
2. Low Temp: Ensure batteries are not on a cold concrete floor; use a thermal pad or heater.

source_alert.name: Charge/Discharge Error
source_alert.description: Logic or Relay failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Logic or Relay failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check if the "Time of Use" or "Peak Shaving" settings are conflicting with the actual load.
2. Inspect DC breakers/fuses between the battery and inverter.

source_alert.name: Battery Fault
source_alert.description: Internal Cell/BMS failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal Cell/BMS failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Power down the entire system and measure the static voltage of each battery module.
2. Contact the battery manufacturer if one module is significantly lower than others.

source_alert.name: DC Injection / DCI
source_alert.description: DC current leaking into AC side.
source_alert.probable_cause: DC current leaking into AC side.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Restart inverter.
2. Check for high-frequency interference or a weak grid neutral.
3. Verify the AC grounding point is solid.

source_alert.name: GFCI / Ground Fault
source_alert.description: Residual/Leakage current to Earth.
source_alert.probable_cause: Residual/Leakage current to Earth.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Caution: Possible shock hazard. Do not touch racking.
2. Perform an Insulation Resistance Test on both AC and DC runs.
3. Look for "weeping" conduits or water in junction boxes.

source_alert.name: SPD AC Fail
source_alert.description: AC Surge Protector "spent."
source_alert.probable_cause: AC Surge Protector "spent."
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Visual Check: Look for a "Red" indicator on the AC surge modules.
2. Replace the plug-in SPD module if the internal fuse has blown.

source_alert.name: SPD DC Fail
source_alert.description: DC Surge Protector "spent."
source_alert.probable_cause: DC Surge Protector "spent."
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for recent lightning or grid surges.
2. Replace the DC SPD module. Note: The inverter may still run, but it is unprotected.

source_alert.name: Protection Trigger
source_alert.description: General Safety Logic trip.
source_alert.probable_cause: General Safety Logic trip.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check for concurrent "IE" codes to find the root cause.
2. Ensure all external emergency stop (E-Stop) buttons are released.

source_alert.name: Firmware Error
source_alert.description: Corrupt image or "Incomplete" update.
source_alert.probable_cause: Corrupt image or "Incomplete" update.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Re-flash the latest stable firmware version.
2. Clear internal cache via Factory Reset if supported.

source_alert.name: Parameter / Config
source_alert.description: Illegal value or "Out of Range" setting.
source_alert.probable_cause: Illegal value or "Out of Range" setting.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Audit the Safety Standard and Battery Voltage settings.
2. Check if a newly added parameter is incompatible with the older hardware version.

source_alert.name: Init Error
source_alert.description: Startup self-check timeout.
source_alert.probable_cause: Startup self-check timeout.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Perform a "Hard Reboot" (AC/DC off for 5 min).
2. Check for stable PV and Grid voltage during the 60s countdown.

source_alert.name: Meter Error
source_alert.description: CT Clamp or Smart Meter mismatch.
source_alert.probable_cause: CT Clamp or Smart Meter mismatch.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check CT orientation: Ensure the arrow points toward the Grid (not the Load).
2. Verify RS485 wiring (A/B) to the meter.

source_alert.name: DRM Error
source_alert.description: Demand Response Mode (DRM0) trip.
source_alert.probable_cause: Demand Response Mode (DRM0) trip.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check the DRM port jumper (often required in Australia/NZ).
2. Verify if the utility is remotely limiting power via the DRM interface.

source_alert.name: Remote Shutdown
source_alert.description: Dry contact or Cloud command.
source_alert.probable_cause: Dry contact or Cloud command.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check the "Remote Shutdown" terminal for a closed/open circuit.
2. Check the App/Portal for an "Active Stop" command.

source_alert.name: Self-Test Failed
source_alert.description: Relay or Logic check failure.
source_alert.probable_cause: Relay or Logic check failure.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ensure the inverter is disconnected from the Grid during the test.
2. If it fails on "Step 3/4," an internal relay is likely stuck.

source_alert.name: Output / Power Limit
source_alert.description: Load mismatch or "Export Limit" hit.
source_alert.probable_cause: Load mismatch or "Export Limit" hit.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check the Export Power Limit in the settings.
2. Verify if the AC load exceeds the inverter's surge capacity (P_{max}).

source_alert.name: Unknown Error
source_alert.description: Undefined firmware state.
source_alert.probable_cause: Undefined firmware state.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Update firmware to see if the code resolves to a known "IE" code.
2. Contact Tech Support with the Serial Number and Internal Logs.

source_alert.name: Guasto CA HCT
source_alert.description: AC HCT sensor/current measurement fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an AC HCT/current measurement fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

source_alert.name: Guasto SPI
source_alert.description: SPI communication/internal interface fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an SPI communication/internal interface fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

source_alert.name: Guasto SCI
source_alert.description: SCI communication/internal interface fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an SCI communication/internal interface fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

source_alert.name: Guasto EEPROMInv
source_alert.description: Inverter EEPROM fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an inverter EEPROM fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

source_alert.name: Relè guasto
source_alert.description: Relay fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates a relay fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

source_alert.name: Errore sample
source_alert.description: Sampling error listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates a sampling/measurement error.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

source_alert.name: Guasto RCD
source_alert.description: Residual current device/detection fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Possible insulation/leakage path between DC/AC circuits and earth.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare l'impedenza tra ciascun ingresso CC e la vite di terra e tra l'uscita CA e la vite di terra. / Check impedance between each DC input and earth screw and between AC output and earth screw.

source_alert.name: ErroreConfigPV
source_alert.description: PV configuration error listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: PV mode configured on display may be incorrect.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare la modalità fotovoltaica inserita a display. / Check the photovoltaic mode entered on the display.

source_alert.name: Rete persa
source_alert.description: Grid lost / no grid condition listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Grid voltage may be absent at the inverter AC connector.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare la tensione di rete al connettore CA dell'inverter. / Check grid voltage at the inverter AC connector.

source_alert.name: Guasto voltAC10M
source_alert.description: 10-minute AC voltage fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Supply voltage or AC line voltage drop may be outside expected range.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare tensione di fornitura e caduta di tensione linea alternata. / Check supply voltage and AC line voltage drop.

source_alert.name: Tensione persa
source_alert.description: Voltage lost condition listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Supply voltage or AC line voltage drop may be outside expected range.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare tensione di fornitura e caduta di tensione linea alternata. / Check supply voltage and AC line voltage drop.

source_alert.name: Frequenza persa
source_alert.description: Frequency lost condition listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Grid voltage/frequency synchronization may be unavailable or unstable.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare tensione di rete. / Check grid voltage.

source_alert.name: Perdita PLL
source_alert.description: PLL loss / grid synchronization loss listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Grid voltage/frequency synchronization may be unavailable or unstable.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare tensione di rete. / Check grid voltage.

source_alert.name: Guasto volt bus
source_alert.description: DC bus voltage fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: PV string voltage/current may be outside inverter nameplate limits.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare che tensioni e correnti delle stringhe fotovoltaiche siano conformi ai dati di targa dell'inverter. / Check PV string voltages and currents against inverter nameplate data.

source_alert.name: Guasto OCP inv
source_alert.description: Inverter over-current protection fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: PV string voltage/current may be outside inverter nameplate limits.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare che tensioni e correnti delle stringhe fotovoltaiche siano conformi ai dati di targa dell'inverter. / Check PV string voltages and currents against inverter nameplate data.

source_alert.name: Guasto DCI OCP
source_alert.description: DC injection over-current protection fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: PV string voltage/current may be outside inverter nameplate limits.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare che tensioni e correnti delle stringhe fotovoltaiche siano conformi ai dati di targa dell'inverter. / Check PV string voltages and currents against inverter nameplate data.

source_alert.name: Guasto volt PV
source_alert.description: PV voltage fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: PV string voltage/current may be outside inverter nameplate limits.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare che tensioni e correnti delle stringhe fotovoltaiche siano conformi ai dati di targa dell'inverter. / Check PV string voltages and currents against inverter nameplate data.

source_alert.name: Guasto RC
source_alert.description: RC fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an RC-related protection/fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Protezione TZ: Verificare l’integrità dei cavi e delle connessioni. / Check cable and connection integrity.

source_alert.name: Guasto SW OCP
source_alert.description: Software over-current protection fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates software over-current protection.
source_alert.recommended_action: Protezione TZ: Verificare l’integrità dei cavi e delle connessioni. / Check cable and connection integrity.

source_alert.name: Protezione TZ
source_alert.description: TZ protection event listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cable or connection integrity issue may be present.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare l’integrità dei cavi e delle connessioni. / Check cable and connection integrity.

source_alert.name: Altro guasto
source_alert.description: Other fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare l'integrità della linea CC e CA dell'inverter. / Check integrity of inverter DC and AC lines.

source_alert.name: Errore MgrEEPROM
source_alert.description: Manager EEPROM error listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an EEPROM manager error.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare l'integrità della linea CC e CA dell'inverter. / Check integrity of inverter DC and AC lines.

source_alert.name: Guasto isolamento
source_alert.description: Insulation fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an insulation fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare l'integrità della linea CC e CA dell'inverter. / Check integrity of inverter DC and AC lines.

source_alert.name: Temperatura alta
source_alert.description: High temperature condition listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Inverter ventilation may be insufficient.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare di aver garantito la corretta ventilazione dell'inverter. / Ensure correct inverter ventilation.

source_alert.name: Errore meter
source_alert.description: Meter error listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Meter-to-inverter communication cable may not work correctly.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare funzionamento cavo di comunicazione tra meter e inverter. / Check operation of the communication cable between meter and inverter.

source_alert.name: Guasto di terra
source_alert.description: Ground fault listed by Peimar.
source_alert.probable_cause: Ground line may be incorrectly installed, or TER detection may have been enabled accidentally.
source_alert.recommended_action: Verificare la corretta installazione della linea di terra. Verificare che non sia stata erroneamente attivata la funzione Rilevamento TER (MENU > OPZIONI (password 2014) > RILEVAMENTO TER). / Check correct ground line installation and verify TER detection was not enabled by mistake.

source_alert.name: Grid OVP
source_alert.description: Grid Over-Voltage. Utility voltage exceeds the inverter's safety threshold.
source_alert.probable_cause: Grid Over-Voltage. Utility voltage exceeds the inverter's safety threshold.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check AC Wiring: Ensure the AC cable isn't too thin (causes voltage drop/rise).
2. Safety Code: Confirm the "Safety Country" matches your local grid standards.

source_alert.probable_cause: Potential Induced Degradation (PID) recovery module is active or faulted.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Night State: This function usually runs at night. If it alarms during the day, the internal PID module is malfunctioning.
2. Settings: Disable PID in the menu to see if the main inverter resumes.

source_alert.name: Liquid Cooling Fault
source_alert.description: (PowerMagic Only) Coolant pressure or flow rate is below the threshold.
source_alert.probable_cause: (PowerMagic Only) Coolant pressure or flow rate is below the threshold.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Pump: Verify if the internal pump is receiving power.
2. Leaks: Inspect the cabinet floor for blue/green coolant residue.
3. Pressure: Ensure the expansion tank is at the correct level (usually 1.0–1.5 bar).

source_alert.name: IsoFault
source_alert.description: Isolation Fault. DC leakage detected from PV or Battery to ground.
source_alert.probable_cause: Isolation Fault. DC leakage detected from PV or Battery to ground.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check PV Strings: Look for damaged insulation or moisture in MC4 connectors.
2. Check Battery: Ensure battery power cables aren't pinched or touching the metal casing.

source_alert.name: BMS Comm Fault
source_alert.description: The inverter has lost communication with the Lithium Battery BMS.
source_alert.probable_cause: The inverter has lost communication with the Lithium Battery BMS.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. BMS Cable: Ensure the RJ45 cable is in the CAN/BMS port (not the Link port).
2. Pinout: Verify the cable pinout matches both SOFAR and your battery brand (often Pins 4 & 5 for CAN).
3. Battery Type: Re-select the correct battery brand in the "Battery Settings" menu.

source_alert.name: Battery OVP
source_alert.description: Battery Over-Voltage. The battery voltage is higher than the allowed limit.
source_alert.probable_cause: Battery Over-Voltage. The battery voltage is higher than the allowed limit.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Configuration: Check if the "Max Charge Voltage" is set correctly for your battery stack.
2. BMS Link: If communication is broken, the inverter may misread the voltage. Fix ID045 first.

source_alert.name: Battery UVP
source_alert.description: Battery Under-Voltage. The battery is too empty to start.
source_alert.probable_cause: Battery Under-Voltage. The battery is too empty to start.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Force Charge: Use the "Grid Charge" function to manually top up the battery.
2. Wake Up: Press the battery module's "Reset" or "Start" button for 5 seconds to wake it from deep sleep.

2. Internal Check: If it persists, an internal output sensor or IGBT module has likely failed.

source_alert.probable_cause: The power module temperature is too high (>85°C).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Fan Test: C&I units use heavy-duty fans. Check if they are blocked by debris or if a fan fuse has blown.
2. Derating: The unit will automatically lower its power output. If it keeps tripping, improve the room's ventilation.
