source_alert.description: Reserved

source_alert.name: Communication Error.
source_alert.description: Communication Error.
source_alert.probable_cause: Communication Error.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Wiring: Check the RS485 wires (A and B).
2. Address: Ensure the meter Modbus ID is set to 001.

source_alert.name: Fast Flash
source_alert.description: Factory Reset.
source_alert.probable_cause: Factory Reset.
source_alert.recommended_action: Triggered if the "Reset" button was held for 10s. You must reconfigure the WiFi.

source_alert.name: General Alarm
source_alert.description: Usually indicates SOC is critically low or a minor sensor mismatch.
source_alert.probable_cause: Usually indicates SOC is critically low or a minor sensor mismatch.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Charge: Immediately provide a charge source to prevent the battery from entering "Deep Sleep."

source_alert.name: Off
source_alert.description: No Inverter Link.
source_alert.probable_cause: No Inverter Link.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Reseat: Unplug and re-plug the stick.
2. Power: Ensure the inverter DC switch is ON (the stick is powered by the inverter).

source_alert.name: Internal Link Fail
source_alert.description: The BDU lost contact with a battery module in the stack.
source_alert.probable_cause: The BDU lost contact with a battery module in the stack.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Pins: Inspect the communication pins between the modules for bends or dirt.
2. Stacking: Ensure the modules are physically clicked in and bolted tightly.

source_alert.name: Waiting
source_alert.description: DC power is present, but the unit is waiting for the grid to stabilize.
source_alert.probable_cause: DC power is present, but the unit is waiting for the grid to stabilize.

source_alert.name: Comm Fail
source_alert.description: Wireless communication to the Smart Hub or Cloud is broken.
source_alert.probable_cause: Wireless communication to the Smart Hub or Cloud is broken.

source_alert.name: Protection State
source_alert.description: Recoverable error (Temperature or Current).
source_alert.probable_cause: Recoverable error (Temperature or Current).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Current: Lower the charge/discharge limit in the inverter.
2. Temp: If below 5°C, the internal heater (if equipped) will activate. Wait for it to warm up.

source_alert.name: Grid Missing
source_alert.description: AC circuit breaker is open or the grid is down. Check the AC cable connection.
source_alert.probable_cause: AC circuit breaker is open or the grid is down. Check the AC cable connection.

source_alert.name: Coding Error
source_alert.description: The BDU cannot assign IDs to the modules.
source_alert.probable_cause: The BDU cannot assign IDs to the modules.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Auto-Coding: Press the "Start" button for 10s. The LEDs should flash purple/blue as it assigns IDs to the stack.

source_alert.name: Coding Mode
source_alert.description: The battery is in the middle of an automatic ID assignment.
source_alert.probable_cause: The battery is in the middle of an automatic ID assignment.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Wait: Do not interrupt this process. It usually happens during the first startup of a parallel system.

source_alert.name: CP Voltage Error
source_alert.description: Control Pilot (Communication) signal failure with the car.
source_alert.probable_cause: Control Pilot (Communication) signal failure with the car.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Reseat: Firmly unplug and re-insert the charging gun into the car.
2. Protocol: Ensure the car is compatible with the Type 2 standard.

source_alert.name: Cannot Login (Web)
source_alert.description: IP Mismatch.
source_alert.probable_cause: IP Mismatch.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Default IP: Connect your PC to LAN4 and go to 192.168.4.11.
2. Credentials: Default is admin / sofar123.

source_alert.name: Input OV/UV
source_alert.description: Grid voltage is outside the operating range (e.g., <180V or >265V).
source_alert.probable_cause: Grid voltage is outside the operating range (e.g., <180V or >265V).
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Utility Check: Usually a grid fluctuation. The charger will auto-recover when voltage stabilizes.

source_alert.name: Inverters "Greyed Out"
source_alert.description: Chain Interruption.
source_alert.probable_cause: Chain Interruption.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Termination: Check if the 120Ohm resistor is active at the last inverter.
2. IDs: Every inverter must have a unique Modbus ID (01, 02, etc.).

source_alert.name: Negative Power
source_alert.description: Reversed CT Clamps.
source_alert.probable_cause: Reversed CT Clamps.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Direction: The arrow on the CT clamp must point toward the Loads/House.
2. Phase Match: Ensure CT1 is clamped to the same phase wire that powers the L1 terminal of the meter.

source_alert.name: No Data on App
source_alert.description: Inverter settings.
source_alert.probable_cause: Inverter settings.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Enable Meter: In the inverter menu, ensure "Smart Meter" is set to "Enable".

source_alert.name: Over-Temp
source_alert.description: Internal components or the charging plug have overheated.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal components or the charging plug have overheated.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Cool Down: Unplug and wait 10 mins. If in direct sun, consider adding a small shade.
2. Deformation: Check the plug pins for signs of melting or discoloration.

source_alert.name: PE Grounding Fault
source_alert.description: The charger's AC input is not correctly earthed.
source_alert.probable_cause: The charger's AC input is not correctly earthed.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Earthing: Ensure the Ground (PE) wire is securely connected.
2. Voltage Check: Measure the voltage between Neutral and Earth; it should be near 0V.

source_alert.name: Residual Current
source_alert.description: Leakage detected in the charger or vehicle (>30mA AC or >6mA DC).
source_alert.probable_cause: Leakage detected in the charger or vehicle (>30mA AC or >6mA DC).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Restart: Toggle the AC breaker OFF/ON and wait 3 mins.
2. Check Cable: Inspect the charging gun for moisture or dirt.
3. Car Side: If it persists, the fault might be in the car’s onboard charger.

source_alert.name: Off
source_alert.description: No Network Connection.
source_alert.probable_cause: No Network Connection.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. WiFi: Router is too far or password changed.
2. 4G: SIM card is out of credit or has no signal.

source_alert.name: Solid On
source_alert.description: Connected to Server.
source_alert.probable_cause: Connected to Server.
source_alert.recommended_action: Normal. Data is being uploaded to SOFAR Cloud.

source_alert.name: Slow Flash
source_alert.description: System OK.
source_alert.probable_cause: System OK.
source_alert.recommended_action: Normal. The logger's internal processor is running.

source_alert.name: Normal
source_alert.description: Unit is producing power and connected to the grid.
source_alert.probable_cause: Unit is producing power and connected to the grid.

source_alert.name: Hardware Fault
source_alert.description: Internal inverter failure. Check the specific error code via the SOFAR Cloud app.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal inverter failure. Check the specific error code via the SOFAR Cloud app.

source_alert.name: System Fault
source_alert.description: General BMS hardware failure or critical safety trip.
source_alert.probable_cause: General BMS hardware failure or critical safety trip.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Hard Reset: Turn off the BDU DC switch, wait 3 minutes, then restart.
2. Check App: Look for specific sub-codes (like "Cell Unbalance").

source_alert.name: IsoFault
source_alert.description: Insulation impedance is too low. Check the DC cables for pinches against the rack.
source_alert.probable_cause: Insulation impedance is too low. Check the DC cables for pinches against the rack.

source_alert.name: SPI Fault
source_alert.description: SPI communication fault
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal communication, memory, controller or firmware/device electronics fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
source_alert.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

source_alert.name: SCI Fault
source_alert.description: SCI communication fault
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal communication, memory, controller or firmware/device electronics fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
source_alert.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

source_alert.name: PV Config Fault
source_alert.description: PV connection setting fault
source_alert.probable_cause: PV/DC input, insulation, bus voltage or DC current condition is outside permitted range.
source_alert.recommended_action: Reset the PV connection. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
source_alert.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

source_alert.recommended_action: Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
source_alert.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

source_alert.name: RCD Fault
source_alert.description: Residual Current Device fault
source_alert.probable_cause: Meter, grounding, leakage current or residual current detection condition is abnormal.
source_alert.recommended_action: Check the impedance of DC input and AC output. Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
source_alert.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

source_alert.name: AC HCT Fault

source_alert.recommended_action: Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.

source_alert.name: TZ Protect Fault
source_alert.description: Over current fault
source_alert.probable_cause: Current has exceeded the permitted limit or current sensor/protection circuit detected abnormal current.
source_alert.recommended_action: Wait for a while to check if it goes back to normal status. Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
source_alert.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

source_alert.recommended_action: Review the auto-test configuration/result and contact support if needed.

source_alert.name: Software BUS Unblance
source_alert.description: Bus not balance
source_alert.probable_cause: PV/DC input, insulation, bus voltage or DC current condition is outside permitted range.
source_alert.recommended_action: Contact with us if this happens frequently.

source_alert.name: PhaseDeltaFault
source_alert.description: Phase fault
source_alert.probable_cause: Grid voltage/frequency/phase condition is outside permitted range or grid connection is unstable.
source_alert.recommended_action: Contact with us if this happens frequently.

source_alert.name: ControlLoopERR
source_alert.description: Controller fault
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal communication, memory, controller or firmware/device electronics fault.
source_alert.recommended_action: Contact with us if this happens frequently.

source_alert.name: DCI Device Fault
source_alert.description: DC Injection current is too high.
source_alert.probable_cause: DC Injection current is too high.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Transient: Usually caused by a grid disturbance. Restart the unit.
2. Hardware: Persistent errors indicate a faulty DCI detection circuit.

source_alert.name: EEPROM Fault
source_alert.description: Internal memory error.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal memory error.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Restart: Cycle the power. If it stays on the screen, the internal control board (DSP) needs replacement.

source_alert.name: BMS Lost
source_alert.description: Communication loss between Inverter and Battery.
source_alert.probable_cause: Communication loss between Inverter and Battery.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Cable: Ensure the CAT5/6 cable is in the BMS/CAN port, not the Meter port.
2. Pinout: SolaX G4 uses a specific pinout (typically Pins 4 & 5 for CAN).
3. Battery Type: Confirm the correct battery (e.g., T-BAT) is selected in the menu.

source_alert.name: BMS_External_Err
source_alert.description: The Battery BMS has reported an internal safety trip.
source_alert.probable_cause: The Battery BMS has reported an internal safety trip.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Battery: Look at the LEDs on the battery pack itself.
2. Restart: Turn off the battery DC switch, wait 2 minutes, then restart the battery before the inverter.

source_alert.name: Overload Fault
source_alert.description: (EPS Mode) Loads exceed the inverter's backup capacity.
source_alert.probable_cause: (EPS Mode) Loads exceed the inverter's backup capacity.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Shed Loads: Turn off high-power items (kettles, AC).
2. Reset: Press 'ESC' on the inverter to clear the latch. If it repeats, the load is too high for the battery/inverter combo.

source_alert.name: EPS OCP Fault
source_alert.description: (EPS Mode) Over-current detected in the backup circuit.
source_alert.probable_cause: (EPS Mode) Over-current detected in the backup circuit.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Short Circuit: Check for a short circuit in your dedicated "backup" consumer unit.
2. Non-linear Loads: Some motors have high inrush currents; try starting them one by one.

source_alert.name: EPS Relay Fault
source_alert.description: The internal changeover relay is stuck or failing.
source_alert.probable_cause: The internal changeover relay is stuck or failing.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Power Cycle: Disconnect PV, Battery, and AC for 5 minutes.
2. Professional Help: If the error persists after a clean boot, the internal relay may need replacement.

source_alert.name: Parallel Fault
source_alert.description: (Parallel Systems) Master/Slave communication error.
source_alert.probable_cause: (Parallel Systems) Master/Slave communication error.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. CAN Link: Check the network cables between the multiple inverters.
2. Settings: Ensure only one unit is set to "Leader" and others to "Follower".

source_alert.name: Tz Fault (Level 1)
source_alert.description: Hardware Over-current Protection.
source_alert.probable_cause: Hardware Over-current Protection.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Instant Trip: Usually caused by a sudden surge. Try a reboot.
2. PV String: Measure the PV voltage to ensure it hasn't spiked above the safety limit.

source_alert.name: BMS_Internal_Err
source_alert.description: Internal communication failure or module mismatch.
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal communication failure or module mismatch.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Cables: Ensure the internal RS485/CAN link cables between modules are clicked in.
2. Restart: Turn off the battery breaker, wait 5 minutes, then restart.
3. Firmware: Often caused by the Inverter and BMS having mismatched versions. Update both via SolaXCloud.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: BMS_Over_Voltage
source_alert.description: Battery voltage is higher than the charging limit.
source_alert.probable_cause: Battery voltage is higher than the charging limit.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Self-Correction: Stop charging. Discharge the battery by running house loads.
2. Cell Balance: Leave the battery at 100% for 24 hours to allow internal balancing.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: BMS_Lower_Voltage
source_alert.description: Battery is critically empty (Deep Discharge).
source_alert.probable_cause: Battery is critically empty (Deep Discharge).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Force Charge: Use the "Force Charge" menu in the inverter's Advanced Settings.
2. PV Wake: If no grid is present, wait for strong sunlight to "jump-start" the BMS.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: BMS_Charge_OCP
source_alert.description: Charge Over-Current Protection.
source_alert.probable_cause: Charge Over-Current Protection.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Settings: Ensure the "Max Charge Current" in the inverter isn't set higher than the battery limit (e.g., 25A–30A).
2. Restart: If it trips instantly, there may be a power cable short.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: BMS_TemHigh / Low
source_alert.description: Battery core temperature is out of range (< -10°C or > 55°C).
source_alert.probable_cause: Battery core temperature is out of range (< -10°C or > 55°C).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Environment: If <0°C, charging will be restricted. If the battery has a built-in heater, it will activate automatically.
2. Ventilation: Check for 300mm clearance around the battery stack.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: BMS_Type_Unmat
source_alert.description: The BMS and Battery Modules are different models.
source_alert.probable_cause: The BMS and Battery Modules are different models.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Check Labels: Ensure all modules in the stack are the same (e.g., all V2 or all V3). You cannot mix generations in one stack.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: BMS_Insulation_Fault
source_alert.description: Leakage detected to the battery casing/earth.
source_alert.probable_cause: Leakage detected to the battery casing/earth.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Plugs: Inspect the orange/black high-voltage plugs for moisture or carbon tracking.
2. Grounding: Ensure the battery rack is earthed to the same point as the inverter.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: LED Color
source_alert.description: Behavior
source_alert.probable_cause: Behavior
source_alert.recommended_action: Meaning
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: Green
source_alert.description: Steady On
source_alert.probable_cause: Steady On
source_alert.recommended_action: Active / Normal. System is operating correctly.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: Green
source_alert.description: Slow Flash (1s On/4s Off)
source_alert.probable_cause: Slow Flash (1s On/4s Off)
source_alert.recommended_action: Idle. Inverter has sent a standby command.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: Orange
source_alert.description: Slow Flash
source_alert.probable_cause: Slow Flash
source_alert.recommended_action: BMS Protection. A non-fatal warning (e.g., Temp High or Volt High).
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: Red
source_alert.description: Steady On (10m) then Flash
source_alert.probable_cause: Steady On (10m) then Flash
source_alert.recommended_action: Fault. Critical error. Check the inverter screen for the specific BMS code.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.name: Green
source_alert.description: Fast Flash (0.3s)
source_alert.probable_cause: Fast Flash (0.3s)
source_alert.recommended_action: Upgrading. Firmware update in progress. Do not power off.
source_alert.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

source_alert.probable_cause: Error Name

source_alert.probable_cause: PID Protection
source_alert.recommended_action: Potential Induced Degradation (PID) module fault.

source_alert.name: ALM 28
source_alert.description: Fan Speed Fault
source_alert.probable_cause: Fan Speed Fault
source_alert.recommended_action: One of the external cooling fans is failing.

source_alert.name: ALM 41
source_alert.description: Liquid Cooling Err
source_alert.probable_cause: Liquid Cooling Err
source_alert.recommended_action: (TRENE Only) Coolant pressure or temperature is abnormal.

source_alert.name: ALM 45
source_alert.description: BMS Comm Fail
source_alert.probable_cause: BMS Comm Fail
source_alert.recommended_action: The PCS (Power Conversion System) lost link with the battery racks.

source_alert.name: ALM 102
source_alert.description: Insulation Low
source_alert.probable_cause: Insulation Low
source_alert.recommended_action: DC insulation resistance is below the safety threshold.

source_alert.name: ALM 120
source_alert.description: AC HCT Fault
source_alert.probable_cause: AC HCT Fault
source_alert.recommended_action: AC Hall Current Transformer (sensor) failure.

source_alert.name: FSS Trigger
source_alert.description: Fire System
source_alert.probable_cause: Fire System
source_alert.recommended_action: (TRENE Only) The Fire Suppression System has been activated or faulted.

source_alert.name: LED State
source_alert.description: Meaning
source_alert.probable_cause: Meaning
source_alert.recommended_action: Detailed Troubleshooting Steps

source_alert.name: Blinking Red
source_alert.description: No Inverter Link
source_alert.probable_cause: No Inverter Link
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Reseat: Unplug and re-plug the dongle firmly.
2. Port Check: Ensure the pins inside the inverter's USB port are not bent or corroded.

source_alert.name: Blinking Yellow
source_alert.description: Connecting to Router
source_alert.probable_cause: Connecting to Router
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. WiFi Signal: The inverter is too far from the router.
2. Credentials: Use the SolaXCloud app to "Re-configure WiFi" with the correct password.

source_alert.name: Blinking Green
source_alert.description: Connecting to Server
source_alert.probable_cause: Connecting to Server
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Internet Access: The router is connected, but there is no internet. Check your ISP.
2. DNS: Some firewalls block SolaX servers. Ensure ports 5279 and 5280 are open.

source_alert.name: Solid Green
source_alert.description: Normal Operation
source_alert.probable_cause: Normal Operation
source_alert.recommended_action: All OK. The system is successfully uploading data to SolaXCloud.

source_alert.name: Symptom / Error
source_alert.description: Possible Reasons
source_alert.probable_cause: Possible Reasons
source_alert.recommended_action: Detailed Troubleshooting Steps

source_alert.name: "COM" Icon Missing
source_alert.description: No RS485 Link.
source_alert.probable_cause: No RS485 Link.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Wiring: Check the Twisted Pair (TP) cable between the Meter (Pins 24/25) and the Inverter.
2. Address: Meter Modbus ID must be 001.

source_alert.name: Negative Wattage
source_alert.description: Reversed CTs.
source_alert.probable_cause: Reversed CTs.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Flip Clamp: The arrow on the CT clamp must point toward the House/Loads.
2. Phase Swap: Ensure the CT labeled "L1" is on the same wire as the "L1" voltage input on the meter.

source_alert.name: Inverter "Meter Fault"
source_alert.description: Logic Mismatch.
source_alert.probable_cause: Logic Mismatch.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Inverter Menu: Go to Settings -> Advanced -> Meter and ensure it is set to "Enable".

source_alert.name: Error / LED
source_alert.description: Meaning
source_alert.probable_cause: Meaning
source_alert.recommended_action: Detailed Troubleshooting Steps

source_alert.name: RUN (Off)
source_alert.description: Power Issue.
source_alert.probable_cause: Power Issue.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. 12V DC: Check the external power adapter or the 12V terminal from the inverter.

source_alert.name: NET (Red)
source_alert.description: Cloud Disconnect.
source_alert.probable_cause: Cloud Disconnect.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Ethernet: Check the LAN cable.
2. Static IP: If your office uses static IPs, log into the DataHub web UI (Default: 192.168.1.10) and enter the gateway details manually.

source_alert.name: COM (Red)
source_alert.description: Sub-device Offline.
source_alert.probable_cause: Sub-device Offline.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Daisy Chain: If one inverter fails, check the RS485 chain.
2. Termination: Ensure the 120Ohm resistor (DIP switch) is turned ON at the last inverter in the line.

source_alert.name: No power
source_alert.description: Inverter no power on LCD
source_alert.probable_cause: PV input is disconnected, DC voltage is too low, or PV polarity is reversed.
source_alert.recommended_action: Check PV input connections. Check DC input voltage (single phase >120V, three phase >350V). Check whether PV+/- is reversed.
source_alert.device_family: Solis S6-GR1P Single Phase Inverter (2.5–6 kW)

source_alert.code: LCD show initializing all the time
source_alert.name: LCD stuck on initializing
source_alert.description: Inverter can not start-up
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal connector on main board / power board or DSP connector may be loose.
source_alert.recommended_action: Check whether the connector on main board or power board is fixed. Check whether the DSP connector to power board is fixed.
source_alert.device_family: Solis S6-GR1P Single Phase Inverter (2.5–6 kW)

source_alert.name: Over grid voltage
source_alert.description: Grid voltage is above the allowed protection limit.
source_alert.probable_cause: AC cable resistance may be too high, grid voltage is too high, or protection limit is not suitable for local grid.
source_alert.recommended_action: Check AC cable resistance and change to bigger size grid cable if needed. Adjust the protection limit only if allowed by the electrical company.
source_alert.device_family: Solis S6-GR1P Single Phase Inverter (2.5–6 kW)

source_alert.recommended_action: Remove all DC input, reconnect and restart inverter one by one. Identify which string causes the fault and check the isolation of the string.

source_alert.name: High DC injection current

source_alert.probable_cause: DC injection current is too high or internal detection/control issue.
source_alert.recommended_action: Restart inverter or contact installer.

source_alert.name: Screen off with DC applied
source_alert.description: LCD screen is off while DC input is still applied; inverter may be internally damaged.
source_alert.probable_cause: Inverter internally damaged or incorrect shutdown operation with DC applied.
source_alert.recommended_action: Do not turn off DC switches while current is high. Wait until solar irradiance reduces and confirm string current is less than 0.5A with a clip-on ammeter, then turn off DC switches. Contact service if needed.
source_alert.device_family: Solis S6-GR1P Single Phase Inverter (2.5–6 kW)

source_alert.name: AFCI module self-detect fault
source_alert.description: AFCI module self-detection fault.
source_alert.probable_cause: AFCI module self-check failed.
source_alert.recommended_action: Restart inverter or connect technician.
source_alert.device_family: Solis S6-GR1P Single Phase Inverter (2.5–6 kW)

source_alert.name: Arcing protection
source_alert.description: Arc detected in DC circuit.
source_alert.probable_cause: DC circuit arc exists or PV connection is abnormal.
source_alert.recommended_action: Check inverter/PV connection for arc and restart inverter.
source_alert.device_family: Solis S6-GR1P Single Phase Inverter (2.5–6 kW)

source_alert.name: DC-OC
source_alert.description: DC Overcurrent
source_alert.probable_cause: DC Overcurrent
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. String Check: A short circuit may exist in the PV strings.
2. Panel Spec: Ensure the panels don't exceed the 16A per MPPT limit of the S6.

source_alert.name: IGBT-OC
source_alert.description: Internal Hardware
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal Hardware
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Restart: Perform a full power cycle (AC/DC off for 5 mins).
2. Fatal: If it returns instantly, the internal power module (IGBT) is likely damaged.

source_alert.name: Batt_Comm_FAIL
source_alert.description: Communication loss. 1. Check the RJ45 cable in the BMS/CAN port.
source_alert.probable_cause: Communication loss. 1. Check the RJ45 cable in the BMS/CAN port.
source_alert.recommended_action:
Communication loss. 1. Check the RJ45 cable in the BMS/CAN port.
2. Ensure the battery is powered ON before the inverter.
3. Verify the CAN cable pinout (Standard Solis pinout: Pin 4 = CAN_H, Pin 5 = CAN_L).

source_alert.name: Wrong Batt Brand
source_alert.description: Setting mismatch. 1. Go to Advanced Settings -> Storage Energy Set -> Battery Select.
source_alert.probable_cause: Setting mismatch. 1. Go to Advanced Settings -> Storage Energy Set -> Battery Select.
source_alert.recommended_action:
Setting mismatch. 1. Go to Advanced Settings -> Storage Energy Set -> Battery Select.
2. Ensure the selected brand (e.g., Pylontech, BYD, SolisStorage) matches your hardware.

source_alert.name: Meter_Comm_FAIL
source_alert.description: No Smart Meter link. 1. Check the RS485 twisted pair wiring between the meter and the inverter.
source_alert.probable_cause: No Smart Meter link. 1. Check the RS485 twisted pair wiring between the meter and the inverter.
source_alert.recommended_action:
No Smart Meter link. 1. Check the RS485 twisted pair wiring between the meter and the inverter.
2. Ensure the meter (Eastron/Acrel) is set to Modbus Address 1.

source_alert.name: No Battery
source_alert.description: Hardware not detected. 1. Check if the battery DC breaker has tripped.
source_alert.probable_cause: Hardware not detected. 1. Check if the battery DC breaker has tripped.
source_alert.recommended_action:
Hardware not detected. 1. Check if the battery DC breaker has tripped.
2. If using a new battery, it may be in "Sleep Mode." Press the battery "Wake" button for 3–5 seconds.

source_alert.name: Backup-Overload
source_alert.description: Too many appliances. 1. Turn off heavy loads (kettles, dryers) connected to the Backup port.
source_alert.probable_cause: Too many appliances. 1. Turn off heavy loads (kettles, dryers) connected to the Backup port.
source_alert.recommended_action:
Too many appliances. 1. Turn off heavy loads (kettles, dryers) connected to the Backup port.
2. Press ESC to clear. If it repeats, the load exceeds the inverter's peak backup rating.

source_alert.name: Batt-Over-Volt
source_alert.description: Voltage too high. 1. Usually self-clears once the battery discharges slightly.
source_alert.probable_cause: Voltage too high. 1. Usually self-clears once the battery discharges slightly.
source_alert.recommended_action:
Voltage too high. 1. Usually self-clears once the battery discharges slightly.
2. If it persists, check if the "Max Charge Voltage" in settings is higher than the battery's limit.

source_alert.name: BMS Alarm
source_alert.description: Internal Battery Error. 1. The battery's own BMS has detected an issue (e.g., cell imbalance).
source_alert.probable_cause: Internal Battery Error. 1. The battery's own BMS has detected an issue (e.g., cell imbalance).
source_alert.recommended_action:
Internal Battery Error. 1. The battery's own BMS has detected an issue (e.g., cell imbalance).
2. Restart the battery first, then the inverter. Check battery LEDs for red "Alarm" lights.

source_alert.name: Healthy
source_alert.description: Healthy. System is normal and communicating.
source_alert.probable_cause: Healthy. System is normal and communicating.
source_alert.recommended_action: No action needed.

source_alert.name: Standby
source_alert.description: Standby. Battery is full and waiting for a load.
source_alert.probable_cause: Standby. Battery is full and waiting for a load.
source_alert.recommended_action: Normal behavior during the day when solar is high.

source_alert.name: Warning
source_alert.description: Warning. Minor issue (e.g., Temp High or Low).
source_alert.probable_cause: Warning. Minor issue (e.g., Temp High or Low).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Temp: Ensure the battery isn't in a hot garage (>45°C).
2. Auto-Recover: It will turn blue once the temp stabilizes.

source_alert.name: Fault
source_alert.description: Fault. Critical error (e.g., Over-current or Cell failure).
source_alert.probable_cause: Fault. Critical error (e.g., Over-current or Cell failure).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Power Cycle: Turn off the battery DC breaker, wait 60s, then restart.
2. App Check: View the specific BMS alarm in SolisCloud.

source_alert.name: Updating
source_alert.description: Updating. Firmware update in progress.
source_alert.probable_cause: Updating. Firmware update in progress.
source_alert.recommended_action: CRITICAL: Do not turn off power. The battery will reboot itself.

source_alert.name: Fan-H / Fan-L
source_alert.description: External or Internal fan is blocked or failing.
source_alert.probable_cause: External or Internal fan is blocked or failing.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Fan Test: Go to Advanced Settings -> Password 0010 -> Fan Test.
2. Inspect: Check for bird nests, grass, or dust in the fan duct.
3. Manual Spin: If a fan is stiff to the touch, it needs replacement.

source_alert.name: EPM Comm. Fail
source_alert.description: Lost link with the Export Power Manager.
source_alert.probable_cause: Lost link with the Export Power Manager.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. RS485: Check the "Daisy Chain" wiring between the multiple inverters and the EPM box.
2. Termination: Ensure the last inverter in the chain has the 120Ohm resistor engaged.

source_alert.name: Steady Red
source_alert.description: Fault. Reseat the dongle. If it stays red, it may need replacement.
source_alert.probable_cause: Fault. Reseat the dongle. If it stays red, it may need replacement.

source_alert.name: Slow Blink (Green)
source_alert.description: Normal. The logger is functioning correctly.
source_alert.probable_cause: Normal. The logger is functioning correctly.

source_alert.name: Off
source_alert.description: No WiFi. Check if the router is on or if the password was changed.
source_alert.probable_cause: No WiFi. Check if the router is on or if the password was changed.

source_alert.name: Fast Blink
source_alert.description: EasyConnect Mode. The dongle is waiting to be configured via the app.
source_alert.probable_cause: EasyConnect Mode. The dongle is waiting to be configured via the app.

source_alert.name: Solid Green
source_alert.description: Connected. WiFi link is stable (Slow blink means data is transferring).
source_alert.probable_cause: Connected. WiFi link is stable (Slow blink means data is transferring).

source_alert.name: Blinking Red
source_alert.description: LAN Data. Data is successfully moving through the Ethernet cable.
source_alert.probable_cause: LAN Data. Data is successfully moving through the Ethernet cable.

source_alert.name: BMS Comm. Fault
source_alert.description: Lost link between Inverter and SBR/SBH battery.
source_alert.probable_cause: Lost link between Inverter and SBR/SBH battery.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Cable Check: Ensure the RJ45 cable is in the "BAT-COM" port (not the LAN port).
2. Power Up: Batteries must be turned ON before the inverter.
3. Pinout: Sungrow uses a specific 1-to-1 pinout for its own batteries.

source_alert.name: Meter Comm. Fault
source_alert.description: The inverter cannot see the Smart Meter (DTSU666).
source_alert.probable_cause: The inverter cannot see the Smart Meter (DTSU666).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. A/B Polarity: Swap RS485 wires A and B on the meter side.
2. ID Check: Ensure the meter is set to ID 1.
3. Termination: For long runs, ensure the 120Ohm resistor is engaged.

source_alert.name: Backup Overload
source_alert.description: Power demand on the Backup port exceeds capacity.
source_alert.probable_cause: Power demand on the Backup port exceeds capacity.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Shed Loads: Turn off high-surge items (pumps, vacuums).
2. Restart: The system usually tries to restart 3 times. If it fails, clear the fault manually in iSolarCloud.

source_alert.name: Meter Energy Abnorm.
source_alert.description: Meter data doesn't match inverter output (CT issue).
source_alert.probable_cause: Meter data doesn't match inverter output (CT issue).
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. CT Direction: Ensure the arrow on CT clamps points toward the Loads.
2. Phase Match: CT1 must be on the same phase as the L1 voltage terminal.

source_alert.name: BMS Comm. Fault
source_alert.description: The inverter lost its "heartbeat" with the battery stack.
source_alert.probable_cause: The inverter lost its "heartbeat" with the battery stack.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Check Pins: Sungrow uses a standard RJ45 cable, but pins 5 (CAN H) and 7 (CAN L) are critical.
2. Voltage Check: Measure the comm-line voltage; it should be between 3.3V – 5.0V.
3. Firmware: Use iSolarCloud to ensure the Inverter, WiNet-S2, and BMS are all on the same version.

source_alert.name: Battery Overvoltage
source_alert.description: One or more cells have exceeded the safety voltage limit.
source_alert.probable_cause: One or more cells have exceeded the safety voltage limit.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Balance Time: This often happens at 99-100% SOC. Leave the battery at 100% (with some solar coming in) to allow the BMS to finish "passive balancing."
2. Hard Reset: If it trips the DC breaker, wait 10 minutes before switching it back on.

source_alert.name: Cell Abnormal
source_alert.description: Significant voltage or temperature difference between modules.
source_alert.probable_cause: Significant voltage or temperature difference between modules.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Calibration: The system may need an "SOC Calibration." (See below).
2. Module Check: Ensure all modules in the stack were manufactured around the same time (Check serial numbers for date codes).

source_alert.name: Low Temp Alarm
source_alert.description: Battery internal temp is below 0°C.
source_alert.probable_cause: Battery internal temp is below 0°C.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Self-Heater: The SBH series has an internal heater. It will draw power to warm itself up. Do not turn the system off during a freeze.

source_alert.name: System Warning
source_alert.description: Minor BMS notification (e.g., SOC jumping or high current).
source_alert.probable_cause: Minor BMS notification (e.g., SOC jumping or high current).
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Monitor: Usually self-clearing. If it repeats, check the DC cable tightness at the Junction Box.

source_alert.name: AFCI Fault
source_alert.description: An electric arc was detected in the DC cabling.
source_alert.probable_cause: An electric arc was detected in the DC cabling.
source_alert.recommended_action: 1. Manual Clear: This is a "Latch" fault. After inspecting MC4 connectors for burns, you must manually clear this in iSolarCloud to restart.

source_alert.name: Fire System
source_alert.description: The Fire Suppression System (Aerosol/Gas) has triggered.
source_alert.probable_cause: The Fire Suppression System (Aerosol/Gas) has triggered.
source_alert.recommended_action:
1. Safety: Evacuate and check for smoke.
2. False Alarm: If no fire, check the "Smoke/Heat Detector" wiring inside the cabinet for a short.
